functions.label: countCommitsBetweenRefs
description: Counts commits reachable from `head` that are not reachable from `base`.
signature: ({ base, head, }: { base: string; head: string; }) => Promise<number>

functions.label: diffStrings
description:
Produces a diff string which is nicely formatted to show the differences between two strings. This will
be a multi-line string so it's generally a good idea to include a `\n` before this first line of the diff
if you are concatenating it with another message.

functions.label: extract
description:
Extract tar and zip archives using a single function, supporting stripComponents
for both archive types, only tested with familiar archives we create so might not
support some weird exotic zip features we don't use in our own snapshot/build tooling

functions.label: hasStagedChanges
description: Returns true when the Git index contains staged changes relative to HEAD.

functions.label: isShallowRepository
description: Returns true when the current repository is a shallow Git checkout.

functions.label: parseAndResolveValidationContract
description: Parses, resolves, and validates validation-contract flags in one call.
signature: ({ profile, scope, testMode, downstream, baseRef, headRef, }: { profile?: string | undefined; scope?: string | undefined; testMode?: string | undefined; downstream?: string | undefined; baseRef?: string | undefined; headRef?: string | undefined; }) =>

children.label:
{
  profile,
  scope,
  testMode,
  downstream,
  baseRef,
  headRef,
}

functions.label: resolveNearestMergeBase
description:
Resolves the closest merge-base candidate between `headRef` and the provided base refs.
"Closest" is the candidate with the smallest ahead count from the merge base to head.
signature: ({ baseRefs, headRef, }: { baseRefs: string[]; headRef?: string | undefined; }) => Promise<

functions.label: startTSWorker

Provide a TS file as the src of a NodeJS Worker with some built-in handling
of std streams and debugging.

functions.label: transformFileStream
description:
Create a transform stream that processes Vinyl fs streams and
calls a function for each file, allowing the function to either
mutate the file, replace it with another file (return a new File
object), or drop it from the stream (return null)

description:
